headline: TJX Missed The Memo and Just Hit $60 Billion in Revenue While the Rest of Retail Struggles
summary: A beloved retailer across its store portfolio, TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX) just crossed a milestone most retailers only dream about: annual revenue surpassing $60 billion for the first time. Shares sit around $154, roughly flat since the February earnings beat, while the broader market has pulled back sharply. Retail investors on Reddit have noticed, and the debate ... TJX Missed The Memo and Just Hit $60 Billion in Revenue While the Rest of Retail Struggles

headline: 1 Consumer Stock to Buy That’s Up More Than 70% Over the Past Year
summary: Ross Stores (ROST) has strong technical momentum. Shares maintain a 100% “Buy” technical opinion from Barchart. ROST is up more than 70% over the past 52 weeks. headline: Ross Stores’ (ROST) Comparable Sales Grew 7% in Q4
summary: Brown Advisory, an investment management company, released its “Brown Advisory Mid-Cap Growth Strategy” fourth-quarter 2025 investor letter. A copy of the letter can be downloaded here. The Strategy lagged the Russell Midcap® Growth Index in the fourth quarter due to stock selection. While the performance was in line with the expectations for the full year. The […]

headline: As many shoppers and retailers struggle, California's Ross Dress for Less is booming
summary: The company behind Ross Dress for Less and other bargain retailers are thriving as consumers look to save money.
